helpCenter.title: Help Center
helpCenter.subtitle: Guides, tips and best practices to get the most out of Ignify.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.description: Set up your workspace, invite your team, and take your first tour.
gettingStarted.content:
Welcome to Ignify! This guide walks you through the first 15 minutes of using the platform so that by the end you have a workspace that understands your brand, a team that can collaborate, and your first AI-generated output.

Step 1 — Complete your brand profile. Go to Business Profile from the sidebar. Fill in your industry, target audience, products/services, and a short brand description. This profile powers every piece of AI-generated content and every marketing plan the system produces, so the more precise you are here, the better everything else will be. If you sell handmade leather goods in Egypt, say exactly that — not just "ecommerce".

Step 2 — Upload your knowledge base. Still inside Business Profile, paste anything you want the AI to know: shipping policy, return policy, frequently asked questions, tone-of-voice rules. The AI reads this before every generation.

Step 3 — Invite your team. Go to Settings → Team. Add marketers as Editor, designers as Contributor, and leadership as Owner. Each role has a clearly scoped permission set, and every action is logged in the audit trail.

Step 4 — Connect a channel. Open Channels and connect at least one of Instagram, WhatsApp, or Facebook. You don't need every channel on day one — pick the one where your customers already live.

Step 5 — Take the product tour. From the dashboard, click the "Tour" button at the top. It walks you through the five main workspaces (Plans, Content, Creative, Ads, Analytics) in about four minutes. You can re-run it any time.

Once done, you're ready to generate your first marketing plan — see the next article.

firstPlan.title: Generate Your First Plan
firstPlan.description: Let AI produce a 90-day marketing plan tailored to your brand.
firstPlan.content:
The Marketing Plan is Ignify's flagship feature. It uses your brand profile to produce a complete 90-day plan covering goals, buyer personas, channel mix, content calendar, KPIs and a full market analysis. Here's how to generate your first one.

1. Open the Marketing Plans workspace from the sidebar. Click "Generate new plan".

2. Confirm the inputs. The form pre-fills from your brand profile, but you can tweak: period length (30/60/90 days), budget tier (lean, balanced, aggressive), and primary objectives (awareness, conversion, retention, launch).

3. Click Generate. The AI will take 60–90 seconds to produce the plan. You'll see a progress indicator with the current stage (market research → personas → calendar → KPIs).

4. Review each section. Every section is regenerable independently — if you love the personas but not the calendar, click the regenerate icon on just that section and the rest stays intact.

5. Collaborate. Leave comments on specific calendar items. Assign calendar items to team members. Approve the plan when ready.

6. Turn the calendar into real content. From any calendar row, click "Draft" to auto-create a content brief in the AI Content workspace, pre-filled with the topic, hook, CTA and target channel. From there you're two clicks away from a finished caption + image.

Tip: generate two or three plan variants with different tones (e.g. "bold" vs "educational") and pick the one that feels most like your brand. The credits cost is small, and side-by-side comparison beats trying to iterate a single plan.

aiContent.title: Create AI Content
aiContent.description: Draft captions, blogs, emails and ads with brand-aware AI.
aiContent.content:
The AI Content workspace generates long-form and short-form copy in Arabic and English, using your brand tone. Here's the fastest path to a publish-ready post.

1. Open AI Content and click New Brief. Pick a format (social post, blog article, email, ad copy) and a channel (Instagram, TikTok, blog, etc). The prompt surface adapts to the format.

2. Fill in the brief. Minimum viable brief: a one-line topic + desired CTA. Better brief: include a hook angle, target persona, length, and 3–5 keywords. Best brief: reference an existing piece of content you loved and say "same tone as this".

3. Generate. You'll get three variants by default. Swipe between them and pick the strongest, or mix sentences from multiple.

4. Refine. Use the inline rewriting tools: "shorter", "more playful", "add emojis", "translate to Arabic", "add hashtags". These are one-click operations that preserve the rest of the copy.

5. Send for review. If your team has an approval workflow, click Send for review and an editor gets notified. If you're the editor, approve and move to Scheduler.

6. Schedule or publish. From the content page, jump to Scheduler to queue the post for the best engagement slot on your chosen channel. Ignify estimates the optimal time based on your past engagement data.

Pro tips: save any well-performing brief as a Template so the whole team can reuse it. Use the "Brand voice score" indicator to make sure generated copy still sounds like you — if it drops below 70%, regenerate.

aiImages.title: Generate AI Images
aiImages.description: Produce product photography and social creatives in seconds.
aiImages.content:
Creative Studio turns text prompts into brand-ready visuals. It supports multiple style presets (editorial, flat-lay, lifestyle, studio, UGC) and aspect ratios for every major channel.

1. Open Creative Studio → Generate. Pick the aspect ratio (1:1 for feed, 9:16 for stories/reels, 16:9 for web banners).

2. Write your prompt. Be concrete: instead of "a nice photo of a wallet" try "top-down flat lay of a dark-brown handmade leather wallet on walnut wood, warm golden afternoon light, moody editorial style, studio aesthetic". Include the subject, angle, lighting, mood and style.

3. Use a style preset. Pick one from the dropdown — this appends tested prompt fragments to your text that steer the model toward the look you want.

4. Generate. You'll receive four variants. Pick the strongest.

5. Refine. Options include: remove background, upscale 2x, variations of this image, inpaint (erase + redraw a region). Each operation is non-destructive — the original is always preserved.

6. Save to library. Good images go into your Assets library where the whole team can reuse them, including directly from the AI Content workspace.

Important: always review generated images for any brand or legal issues before using externally — e.g. logos, faces of real people, or trademarked items. Ignify warns you when it detects these but cannot catch every case.

socialAccounts.title: Connect Social Accounts
socialAccounts.description: Link Instagram, Facebook, TikTok and more to schedule and measure.
socialAccounts.content:
Connecting social accounts lets Ignify both publish on your behalf and read engagement metrics to improve recommendations. All tokens are encrypted at rest and scoped to the minimum permissions required.

Instagram / Facebook: use Channels → Connect → Instagram. You'll be redirected to Meta to authorize. Required permissions: pages_show_list, pages_read_engagement, instagram_basic, instagram_content_publish. Your Instagram account must be a Business or Creator account linked to a Facebook Page.

TikTok: Channels → Connect → TikTok. The TikTok for Business OAuth flow will open. You need a TikTok Business account.

WhatsApp: two options. (a) WhatsApp Business Cloud API via Meta — recommended for volume. (b) QR pairing via the built-in connector — faster for small accounts.

Google / YouTube: OAuth via Google, scoped to YouTube upload + analytics read.

Troubleshooting: if a token expires, you'll see a red banner in the Channels page. Click Reconnect. If Meta rejects the permission request, make sure your app is out of Development mode and the page you're connecting is managed by the same user that is logged into Ignify.

Privacy: you can disconnect a channel at any time. Disconnecting revokes the token, stops all scheduled posts on that channel, and purges stored metrics after 30 days.

team.title: Manage Your Team
team.description: Invite teammates, assign roles and track activity.
team.content:
Ignify is built for teams of 3–30 people collaborating on marketing. Every action is scoped to a role and logged to the audit trail.

Roles:
- Owner: full access including billing, plan upgrades, data deletion.
- Admin: everything except billing.
- Editor: create/edit/publish content, plans and creatives. Cannot change settings or invite users.
- Contributor: create drafts and submit for review. Cannot publish directly.
- Viewer: read-only.

Inviting: Settings → Team → Invite. Enter email, pick a role, and optionally restrict to specific workspaces (e.g. only the Ads workspace for an agency contractor). The invitee receives a bilingual email with a one-click accept link. Invitations expire after 7 days.

Audit log: Settings → Audit Log shows every change — who did what, when, with the diff. Filter by user, resource type, or date range. Exportable as CSV.

Transferring ownership: a current Owner can promote another user to Owner at any time. There can be multiple Owners. To remove yourself as Owner, promote someone else first.

Single sign-on: available on the Enterprise plan. We support Google Workspace, Microsoft Entra, and any SAML 2.0 IdP. Contact support to enable.

Best practice: give agency contractors Contributor access, not Editor, so their work is always reviewed by your team before going live.

billing.title: Billing & Plans
billing.description: Manage your subscription, credits and invoices.
billing.content:
Ignify billing has two components: a monthly subscription (Starter / Professional / Enterprise) and a credit balance for AI usage. Most text operations are 1–5 credits; image generation is 10–40 credits depending on resolution.

Changing plan: Settings → Billing → Change plan. Upgrades are prorated and take effect immediately. Downgrades take effect at the end of the current billing cycle — you keep your higher limits until then.

Credits: your plan includes a monthly credit grant. Top-ups are available in packs of 1,000 / 5,000 / 20,000. Credits never expire and top-up credits are used before the monthly grant, so you don't lose unused top-ups.

Payment methods: Stripe (global cards), Paymob (Egypt — cards, Fawry, wallets), PayTabs (GCC — cards, mada, Apple Pay). We add methods based on demand; ask support if you need a specific provider.

Invoices: all invoices are available under Settings → Billing → Invoices. VAT-compliant for Egypt and KSA. You can add your company tax ID under Settings → Billing → Tax info.

Refunds: within 14 days of a new subscription for Starter/Professional, no questions asked. Enterprise is governed by your signed contract.

Failed payments: we retry three times over 7 days before suspending the account. You'll get email warnings before any disruption. Your data is retained for 30 days after suspension, giving you time to resolve the issue without data loss.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.content:
Ignify is keyboard-friendly. Press ? anywhere to open the shortcut sheet.

Global:
- Cmd/Ctrl + K — command palette (jump anywhere, search anything).
- G then D — go to Dashboard.
- G then P — go to Marketing Plans.
- G then C — go to AI Content.
- G then I — go to AI Creative (images).
- G then A — go to Analytics.
- G then S — go to Settings.
- / — focus search.
- ? — open shortcut sheet.
- Esc — close any modal.

Content editor:
- Cmd/Ctrl + Enter — generate / regenerate.
- Cmd/Ctrl + S — save draft.
- Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + P — publish (if you have permission).
- Alt + 1/2/3 — switch between variants.
- Cmd/Ctrl + B — bold, Cmd/Ctrl + I — italic.

Calendar / scheduler:
- J / K — move selection down / up.
- T — go to today.
- W / M — switch to week / month view.
- N — new post at selected slot.
- Del — delete selected post.

Inbox:
- E — archive, R — reply with AI suggestion, Shift + R — reply manually.
- 1–5 — apply quick label.

Arabic keyboard: all shortcuts use physical key positions, so they work identically whether you're typing in Arabic or English layout.

Accessibility: full screen-reader support, focus indicators, and respect for prefers-reduced-motion. If a shortcut conflicts with your assistive tech, you can rebind it in Settings → Preferences → Shortcuts.
